findings.text: Redacted draft uses '[REDACTED]' for matriculation number instead of '[Matriculation Number]' as specified in redaction annotations, potentially leaving the identifier insufficiently protected.
Redacted draft references NAP-SRC-0013 (NHS form) without redacting personal identifiers from that source as required by annotations, risking re-identification.
Full name 'Anfal Elbushra' is present in original draft but absent from redacted draft, consistent with redaction annotations.
Student number field is omitted in redacted draft, which is acceptable per redaction annotations.

proposed_tasks.instructions: Update the redacted draft art-0b9032029d0c46b7906476b52bab79e9 to replace '[REDACTED]' with '[Matriculation Number]' for the matriculation number, and ensure that any reference to NAP-SRC-0013 includes a note that personal identifiers have been redacted from that source, or remove the reference if the source cannot be safely included.
proposed_tasks.title: Fix redaction placeholders in complaint draft

redaction_flags.reason: Matriculation number placeholder does not match annotation specification; should be '[Matriculation Number]'.

risk_flags.text: Matriculation number placeholder '[REDACTED]' does not match annotation requirement '[Matriculation Number]', potentially leaving identifier exposed.
NHS form reference NAP-SRC-0013 may contain unredacted personal identifiers, violating annotation requirement.

summary: Redacted draft contains residual identifiers: full name 'Anfal Elbushra' appears in the original draft but is absent from the redacted draft; however, the redacted draft retains the matriculation number placeholder '[REDACTED]' which is insufficiently specific and may not adequately protect the identifier. The redaction annotations require replacement of the matriculation number with '[Matriculation Number]', but the redacted draft uses '[REDACTED]' instead. This discrepancy is a material privacy defect. Additionally, the redacted draft omits the student number field entirely, which is acceptable, but the NHS form reference in the evidence section still mentions 'NAP-SRC-0013' without redacting the form's personal identifiers as required by annotations. No other direct identifiers found.

uncertainties.text: Whether the placeholder '[REDACTED]' for matriculation number is materially unsafe depends on the context of the complaint/support pack; it may still allow re-identification if combined with other data.
